analyses.headline: PostPrime acquires M&A brokerage firm Infinity Life, entering new growth domain.
analyses.summary: PostPrime, operator of the financial and economic information platform "PostPrime," is acquiring Infinity Life, an M&A and real estate brokerage firm, making it a wholly-owned subsidiary. This strategic move marks PostPrime's entry into the M&A brokerage sector, leveraging its existing user base to offer practical, value-added services beyond information provision. The acquisition aims to establish a dual revenue stream and capitalize on the growing demand for business succession M&A in Japan.
analyses.significance: This acquisition diversifies PostPrime's business model beyond content and advertising, tapping into the structurally growing Japanese business succession M&A market. It enhances user monetization by converting platform engagement into transactional services, potentially improving PostPrime's overall revenue stability and growth trajectory.
key_facts: PostPrime decided to acquire 100% of Infinity Life's shares on June 17, 2026, with a planned execution date of July 1, 2026.
The total acquisition cost is JPY 105 million, comprising JPY 100 million for shares and JPY 5 million for advisory fees.
Infinity Life reported JPY 129 million in revenue and JPY 24 million in ordinary profit for the fiscal year ended March 2025.
PostPrime's SNS platform currently boasts approximately 430,000 users, which will serve as a channel for M&A deal sourcing.
analyses.investor_takeaway: Investors should note PostPrime's strategic pivot to diversify revenue streams and capitalize on the growing M&A brokerage market, potentially improving monetization of its existing user base and enhancing long-term growth prospects.

analyses.headline: CEO-related entity increases stake in eWeLL, crossing 5% ownership threshold.
analyses.summary: An entity associated with eWeLL's CEO has acquired additional shares from the CEO, bringing its total ownership to 5.64%. This transaction is classified under regulations governing significant share accumulations, signaling a consolidation of control by the CEO's related party.
analyses.significance: This move enhances the stability of the company's shareholding structure by strengthening the CEO's influence through a dedicated entity, potentially impacting future strategic decisions and governance.
key_facts: iBow Inc., an entity related to eWeLL's CEO, acquired 60,000 shares.
The acquisition increases iBow Inc.'s total holding to 860,000 shares.
Post-acquisition, iBow Inc. holds 5.64% of eWeLL's total voting rights.
iBow Inc. states its intention to be a long-term, stable shareholder.
analyses.investor_takeaway: Investors should note the increased concentration of ownership by a CEO-affiliated entity, reinforcing management's long-term commitment and control.
